recent_examples.title: Friction as Signal
recent_examples.idea: The idea that reducing friction (as seen in the 20% completion rate increase) actually *improves* signal by allowing the right candidates to finish the process.

recent_examples.idea: We are using AI to "move words from one place you don't read them to another place you don't read them." This identifies a fundamental decoupling of information *processing* from human *understanding*...

recent_examples.title: Model Asymmetry:
recent_examples.idea: The idea that using "the absolute dumbest models" (4o-mini) in multiple small steps can produce a more "intelligent" and certainly more resilient output than single-shot high-end models.

recent_examples.title: Culture as the Operating System
recent_examples.idea: If culture is the OS of a company, then "output-centricity" is a bug that causes "innovation crashes."

recent_examples.title: State Escalation
recent_examples.idea: Could the manifest system be used to trigger automatic "emergency" alerts if certain keywords (e.g., "urgent," "crisis") are detected in B11 (Risks & Flags)?

recent_examples.title: Productivity as "Home":
recent_examples.idea: The idea that a productivity tool isn't just a utility, but a "home" that reflects one's vulnerability and life history. Building a "room" in software as an act of self-actualization.

recent_examples.title: The Scaffold Offer:
recent_examples.idea: By offering his own N5 repo, V is attempting to bypass the "cold start" problem of industrial-grade AI hardware, treating system-design as a gift-economy lever.
